name: Bhai Surjit Singh (Long Island)
body: Bhai Surjit Singh Ji is currently one of the finest exponents of the Gurmat Sangeet tradition. He was born in the village of Buggawala which is located in Haridwar, Uttarakhand. He began learning Gurmat Sangeet from Giani Hardit Singh Ji, principal of Gurmat Vidyalya Gurdwara Rakab Ganj Sahib. He stayed under the guidance of Giani Hardit Singh Ji for around one year before Giani Ji passed away. The responsibility of Bhai Surjit Singh Ji along with the rest of the Vidyalya was passed on to Giani Dyal Singh Ji, who became the successor of the Vidyalya. In 1972, Giani Dyal Singh Ji requested Bhai Surjit Singh Ji to accompany Giani Ji’s maternal uncle, Bhai Lal Singh (Meerut) for a tour in Southeast Asia. They spent about 5 to 6 months traveling around Thailand, Malaysia and Singapore during this tour. Once he returned to Delhi, he joined the ‘jatha’ of Bhai Chattar Singh Ji and accompanied him on the side harmonium for 9 years before forming his own ‘jatha’ in 1981. He toured Nairobi for about 4 to 5 months with his newly formed ‘jatha’ and then went to the UK. Following his tours, he returned to Delhi and performed Kirtan regularly in Gurdwara Kirti Nagar. Bhai Surjit Singh Ji also made two trips to Canada and USA in between. The first was in 1982 and then again in 1985. In 1990, he settled permanently in Long Island, New York and has been serving the Sangat in the US for over three decades. Currently, he receives bookings from various Gurdwaras throughout the year and delights the Sangat with his deep knowledge and skills in Gurmat Sangeet. He is particularly adept in singing ‘Partaals’. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Surjit Singh Ji, please contact us.
The following recordings were obtained from the Gurmat Sangeet Project website. Please visit their page by clicking on the following link http://www.gurmatsangeetproject.com/
